heroHeadline: Balikatan 2026 Day 8: US-Philippines-New Zealand-Australia Execute HIMARS Counter-Landing Drills at Palawan; PLA Releases YJ-20 Hypersonic Missile Footage as Direct Counter-Signal
heroSubtitle: Day 8 of Balikatan 2026 (April 27) brought a direct convergence of allied capability-building and Chinese counter-signaling: US, Philippine, New Zealand, and Australian service members conducted a counter-landing live-fire exercise at Long Point Beach, Barangay Aporawan, Aborlan, Palawan — featuring HIMARS rocket systems and unprecedented unmanned systems/drone integration in a combined amphibious denial scenario overlooking the South China Sea. Simultaneously, China's PLA released footage of its Type 055 destroyer Zunyi firing a YJ-20 hypersonic anti-ship cruise missile during exercises east of Luzon — a weapon with estimated 1,000–1,500 km range and Mach 10 terminal speed, capable of engaging every allied naval formation participating in Balikatan 2026. South China Morning Post described the footage release as timed specifically to 'heat up South China Sea as Balikatan drills continue,' completing a counter-posture that now includes the Liaoning carrier group, Task Group 107 live-fire drills (April 24–25), Type 076 Sichuan drone carrier sea trials, and the 352-meter Scarborough Shoal floating barrier. Malaysia issued its clearest statement yet against any weaponization of the Strait of Malacca, with Prof. Dr. Salawati Mat Basir (UKM) declaring under UNCLOS the strait 'cannot be treated like a private road' — directly resisting geopolitical pressure generated by the Iran-Hormuz crisis. The IAMD phase at Naval Station Leovigildo Gantioqui (NSLG), Zambales continues through April 29. The 48th ASEAN Summit (May 5–9, Cebu) and Japan's SINKEX of BRP Quezon are the remaining milestone events for the exercise window.
footerNote: Data sourced from CSIS AMTI, Philippine DFA/AFP/PCG, US Dept. of Defense/DVIDS, USNI News, Reuters, AP, South China Morning Post, Al Jazeera, Rappler, GMA News, Manila Bulletin, Manila Times, Tribune Philippines, Philstar, Stars and Stripes, The Diplomat, US News, Free Malaysia Today, Radio Free Asia, Canadian Inquirer, Arab News, Defence Security Asia, The Star (Malaysia)
